description: Cessative 'te' catches an action or state at the point it stops. This differs from 'ki' (PFV): an activity can stop without reaching completion. The inchoative 'pa' (INCH) covers the other boundary, when the event begins.
articulatory_notes: The tongue tip meets the backs of the upper teeth for dental 't' and releases into true-mid front 'e'. The syllable is fully stressed.
sound_symbolism: The dental closure gives 'te' a brief edge, then releases into the vowel. That compact settling can recall an event coming to rest.
usage_notes: The aspect position follows tense, so 'to te' means stopped and 'so te' means will stop. The particle can mark a deliberate stopping or an event that simply ceases; it adds no separate claim about the cause.
